title: No hiding of `--cidfile` for remote clients
body:
### Feature request description

In #25512 it was noticed that, of the commands supporting the `--cidfile` flag, `pause`, `restart`, `unpause` and `stop` hide it on remote clients, whereas `rm` and `kill` (and soon `exec`) don't.

@Luap99 and I are of the opinion that there is no reason to hide the `--cidfile` flag for remote clients, since the file is simply read on the client side and that's it.

See also #25612.

### Suggest potential solution

Do not mark `cidfile` hidden for the affected commands.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body: @findesgh are you interested in contributing this by PR?
If there is no objection on the matter, sure. I'll watch this issue for a while and if no one objects, I'll do it.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
